cards.back: A branch of digital electronics that deals with the analysis, design, and implementation of digital circuits using logic gates and other combinational circuits.
A digital circuit that produces an output based on a combination of inputs, without any memory or feedback loop.
A systematic approach to designing digital circuits, involving the creation of a truth table, K-maps, and the implementation of logic gates and other combinational circuits.
A combinational circuit that performs binary addition and subtraction operations, using a combination of logic gates and a carry mechanism.
A combinational circuit that converts a binary code into a set of signal values, representing the decoded information.
